channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: arXiv Implements Measures Against Unchecked AI-Generated Errors in Research Papers
item.description: The preprint platform arXiv has introduced strict measures against papers containing AI-generated errors, implementing a one-year ban for authors whose submissions include unverified AI content. This ...

item.title: Four Killed and Hundreds Arrested in Kenya Over Fuel Price Protests
item.description: On Monday, Kenya experienced widespread protests as citizens expressed frustration over the rising fuel costs, which have also affected transport and the prices of basic commodities. Interior Cabinet ...

item.title: Congress MP Randeep Surjewala summoned in Himanta Sarma's wife passport investigation
item.description: The Assam Crime Branch has formally summoned Congress leader and MP Randeep Singh Surjewala to appear in Guwahati on Saturday, May 23, regarding the ongoing investigation into passport-related discrep...

item.title: Drake’s ICEMAN Fails to Capture Former Brilliance
item.description: Drake's latest release, ICEMAN, marks the first installment in his ambitious 2026 comeback trilogy, but it struggles to deliver a compelling narrative or musical innovation. The album spans over two a...

item.title: Community and Team-Based Initiatives Improving Healthcare Across South Africa
item.description: While the National Health Insurance (NHI) Act waits for a Constitutional Court ruling, healthcare innovation is happening across South Africa. From remote villages to urban hospitals, teams are creati...

item.title: TPDC prepares for next stage of Eyasi-Wembere oil exploration after seismic survey
item.description: The Tanzania Petroleum Development Corporation (TPDC) is set to move forward with the Eyasi-Wembere oil exploration project next month as the ongoing geophysical survey nears 78 percent completion. Th...

item.title: EPRA Revises Fuel Prices: Diesel Price Falls, Kerosene Cost Rises in Nairobi and Mombasa
item.description: The Energy and Petroleum Regulatory Authority (EPRA) has issued fresh fuel prices effective from Tuesday, May 19, to Sunday, June 14, 2026, following tensions with matatu operators who went on strike ...

item.title: Petrol and Diesel Prices Rise Amid Global Crude Surge, Opposition Criticises Government
item.description: On Tuesday, the Congress party sharply criticised the Narendra Modi-led central government following an increase of approximately 90 paise per litre in petrol and diesel prices. This marks the second ...

item.title: Early Media Focus on AOC and Ro Khanna Ahead of 2028 Democratic Primary
item.description: As the 2028 presidential race remains more than a year away, media outlets are already highlighting potential Democratic contenders, particularly Reps. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez (AOC) and Ro Khanna. AO...

item.title: Tribeca Gallery Displays Epstein Documents with Prominent Trump Photo
item.description: A new art installation in Manhattan’s Tribeca neighborhood highlights the connections between former President Donald Trump and the late financier Jeffrey Epstein. The gallery, named the 'Donald J. Tr...

item.title: Putin visits China to reinforce strategic partnership with Xi
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin has arrived in Beijing for talks with Chinese President Xi Jinping, aiming to emphasise that the Russia-China relationship remains strong and stable, even after a visi...

item.title: SA faces wet and windy conditions with scattered thunderstorms across the country
item.description: South Africans should brace for a chilly and unsettled Tuesday, as wet, cold and windy weather spreads across much of the country. The South African Weather Service has issued a Yellow Level 2 warning...

item.title: Montreal Canadiens advance to Eastern Conference final after overtime win against Buffalo
item.description: On Monday night, the Montreal Canadiens defeated the Buffalo Sabres 3-2 in overtime, securing their place in the Eastern Conference final. The game was a rollercoaster, with Montreal taking an early l...

item.title: EPRA Adjusts Fuel Prices: Diesel Reduced, Kerosene Hiked Amid Public Transport Concerns
item.description: The Energy and Petroleum Regulatory Authority (EPRA) announced new fuel prices in Kenya, effective from May 19 to June 14, 2026. Diesel prices have been reduced by KSh 10.06 per litre, now retailing a...

item.title: STAYC Announces Fan Concert in Manila This September
item.description: The popular K-pop girl group STAYC is set to hold a fan concert in Manila this coming September, much to the excitement of their Filipino fans. The announcement, made by ABS-CBN Entertainment, highlig...

item.title: Niverville Nightha-W-ks Win the Centennial Cup National Championship
item.description: The Niverville Nightha-W-ks have captured the Centennial Cup, emerging as the national champions in Canadian junior hockey. This victory marks a significant achievement for the team and their communit...

item.title: Number of Chinese vessels in West Philippine Sea drops to 27, AFP reports
item.description: The Armed Forces of the Philippines (AFP) reported a decrease in the number of Chinese vessels in the West Philippine Sea (WPS) over the past week. From May 12 to 18, the AFP monitored a total of 27 C...

item.title: Three killed in shooting at San Diego mosque; two teenage suspects dead
item.description: A tragic shooting occurred at the Islamic Center of San Diego, California, resulting in the deaths of three individuals, including a security guard, and two teenage suspects aged 17 and 18 who apparen...

item.title: Victor Wembanyama leads Spurs to double-overtime win over Thunder in Game 1 of Western Conference Finals
item.description: In Game 1 of the Western Conference Finals, the San Antonio Spurs defeated the Oklahoma City Thunder 122-115 in a double-overtime thriller, ending Oklahoma City's playoff unbeaten streak that had last...

item.title: Sony Cancels Plans to Release Major PlayStation Single-Player Games on PC
item.description: Sony has decided to halt the release of its major single-player PlayStation games on PC, according to Bloomberg journalist Jason Schreier. Hermen Hulst, head of PlayStation Studios, reportedly informe...
